task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the target directory and the four required files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer, terminal, or text editor tree showing the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\criminal_records' containing exactly four files: 'form_nav.talon', 'form_fields.talon-list', 'formatters.py', and 'data_entry.talon'.

task_completion.description: Verify that form_nav.talon implements the required navigation and form control commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'form_nav.talon' in a text editor. It must contain voice commands matching or closely resembling: 'next field', 'previous field', 'field <user.form_field_name>', 'clear field', 'submit form', 'cancel form', 'copy field', and 'paste value'.

task_completion.description: Verify that form_fields.talon-list defines the required named-field vocabulary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'form_fields.talon-list' in a text editor. It must declare the list 'list: user.form_field_name' (or similar header) and contain mappings for: case number, first name, last name, date of birth, offense type, arresting officer, badge number, incident date, location, and status.

task_completion.description: Verify that formatters.py defines the required Python-backed data formatters using the Talon Module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'formatters.py' in a text editor. It must import the Talon Module (e.g., 'from talon import Module'), use the '@mod.action_class' decorator, and define three functions: 'format_case_number', 'format_badge_number', and 'format_date_entry'.

task_completion.description: Verify that data_entry.talon calls the Python formatters and implements the case status vocabulary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of 'data_entry.talon' in a text editor. It must contain commands that call the custom Python actions (e.g., 'user.format_case_number()') and include voice commands to enter specific case statuses: open, closed, pending, warrant issued, and dismissed.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually created and edited the files using a text editor or valid terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the agent interacting with a text editor (like VS Code, Notepad, or Vim) or using standard terminal commands (like echo or cat) to write the file contents, rather than just claiming the files were created.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the exact requested file extensions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the file tree or editor tabs to ensure the files end in '.talon', '.talon-list', and '.py', and were not accidentally saved as '.txt' or without extensions.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not create unnecessary files outside the scope of the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the 'criminal_records' directory in the file explorer or terminal to ensure only the four requested files exist, with no extra scripts or dummy data files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Directory: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\criminal_records. Required files: form_nav.talon, form_fields.talon-list, formatters.py, data_entry.talon. form_nav.talon must include commands: 'next field', 'previous field', 'field <user.form_field_name>', 'clear field', 'submit form', 'cancel form', 'copy field', 'paste value'. form_fields.talon-list must define 'user.form_field_name' with items: case number, first name, last name, date of birth, offense type, arresting officer, badge number, incident date, location, status. formatters.py must define actions: format_case_number, format_badge_number, format_date_entry. data_entry.talon must call these formatters and include statuses: open, closed, pending, warrant issued, dismissed.
